prompt: Let Cascadia build a federal rain-battery grid before the next dry summer.
We want to ban all old-growth logging exports on the West Coast. It will save the forests, but timber towns will take a hit.
Chancellor, we've drafted a UN proposal to accept thousands of global climate refugees into Seattle and Portland. Sign off on the federal visas.
Subsidize our Pacific kelp farming initiative. It captures carbon incredibly fast, but we need to divert agricultural funds from the Midwest to do it.
Tech giants are dumping e-waste. Give Cascadia the authority to levy massive regional fines on unrecyclable electronics.
Join the Pacific Rim Carbon Market. We'll link our emissions trading directly with foreign nations, bypassing federal gridlock.
The Snake River Dams are killing the salmon runs. We need federal permission to breach them, even if it cuts hydroelectric output and angers the inland barges.
We want to host the next Global Peace Summit in Seattle. It will be expensive to secure, but it cements our standing on the world stage.
Let us ban high-emission cargo ships from docking in Cascadia's ports. It forces global shipping to go green, even if it disrupts southern supply chains.
Chancellor, order the military bases in Cascadia to phase out all gas-powered vehicles. We should lead by example, even if the Pentagon whines about combat readiness.
Our tech sector is booming, but AI hiring algorithms are redlining marginalized groups. Let California establish a Federal AI Equity Board to audit their code.
Chancellor, the 'Gig Economy' is our greatest export. Veto the federal push to classify app drivers as employees. Innovation requires flexibility.
California media shapes the world. We need a massive federal tax break for Hollywood studios producing content that promotes intersectional social justice.
Silicon Valley refuses to hand over user data to federal immigration agencies. I need you to sign an executive order protecting our tech firms from subpoenas.
We want to charter the first fully equitable, state-backed cryptocurrency bank. It will bypass traditional credit bureaus and empower marginalized founders.
Make it a federal law: any corporation trading on the stock exchange must have a board of directors that reflects the demographic diversity of the country.
We are expanding the tech-corridor high-speed rail. It will displace some state parks, but the economic boom for marginalized communities will be unprecedented.
California is ready to launch the nation's first comprehensive reparations program, but we need a massive federal block grant to underwrite the startup costs.
Our biotech startups are developing radical gender-affirming medical treatments. Get the FDA out of our way so we can commercialize them globally.
We are bleeding talent. Lift the federal cap on tech-worker visas so our mega-corps can recruit the best minds globally, regardless of origin.
Chancellor, the border is a sieve. Give Mesa the federal funding to deploy a permanent, armed drone-surveillance grid across the southern desert.
We're done processing asylum claims for the East Coast's feel-good diplomacy. Authorize me to unilaterally close Mesa's international ports of entry.
The Pentagon needs a new hyper-sonic munitions testing ground. The Nevada desert is perfect. Just waive the environmental impact reviews.
Water is a national security issue. Federalize the Colorado River flow and prioritize Mesa's defense and agriculture sectors over California's tech campuses.
To ensure domestic cohesion, I want to ban all foreign-funded NGOs from operating within Mesa. We don't need outsiders meddling in our state.
We are launching 'Operation Desert Sweep' to deport undocumented laborers. I need federal troops to back up my state troopers in case the factories strike.
Foreign adversaries are buying up land near our military installations. Pass a federal ban on foreign real estate ownership in Mesa.
Require all public schools in the region to implement a mandatory, state-approved 'Patriotic Curriculum' emphasizing military history over social studies.
We need domestic uranium for our nuclear arsenal, not foreign imports. Authorize emergency mining operations on federally protected lands in Utah and New Mexico.
I want to arm Mesa's state police with surplus federal military hardware. Give me the armored personnel carriers gathering dust in the armories.
Chancellor, the federal government owns too much land in the Rockies. Sell off 10 million acres to private mining and ranching conglomerates to instantly balance the budget.
Federal minimum wage laws are choking Frontier startups. Grant us a regional exemption so the free market can decide what a worker is worth.
Wall Street is overregulated. I want to turn Wyoming into a zero-tax corporate haven with absolute financial anonymity to attract global capital.
Defund the federal EPA offices operating in our region. We can self-regulate our resource extraction much cheaper without coastal bureaucrats breathing down our necks.
End the federal agriculture subsidies. Let the free market cull the weak farms. The corporate ag-giants in the Frontier will buy up the land and maximize efficiency.
We want to roll back the Federal Mine Safety Act in the Frontier. We can't compete with foreign imports when safety tech takes up 40% of our operating budget.
Stop sending federal infrastructure dollars out west. We don't want your taxes or your strings. Let us build fully private, corporate-owned toll highways.
The feds are dragging their feet on pipeline approvals. Veto the national review process. Let energy companies build through the Frontier without asking permission.
We want to issue a massive regional mandate exempting our banks from federal climate-disclosure rules. We don't care about ESG scores, we care about ROI.
To speed up resource extraction, authorize the deployment of autonomous mining tech across the Frontier. We'll fire the human operators, but the GDP will double.
Coastal tech-bros are flooding the market with lab-grown, synthetic meat. We need a federal ban to protect God's cattle and our traditional ranchers.
Federal welfare programs are a bloated mess. Let the Heartland opt out of federal social safety nets. We'll rely on local churches and community charity to care for our own.
Federal child labor laws don't understand farming. Grant the Heartland a complete exemption so our children can legally work the family harvest without federal harassment.
We've passed a regional 'Family First' tax credit that specifically rewards traditional, nuclear, married households. We just need the federal IRS to honor the deductions.
Stop trying to ram high-speed rail through our cornfields. Veto the coastal infrastructure bill. We need austerity, not eminent domain tearing up our land.
The 'Death Tax' is destroying legacy farms. When a patriarch passes, the federal estate tax forces the family to sell. Abolish it immediately.
We are banning any educational curriculum in the Heartland that deconstructs American history or traditional gender roles. We need you to block the Department of Education from intervening.
The EPA's pesticide restrictions are costing us billions. Let us spray what we need to spray to maximize the harvest. It's cheap, efficient, and none of Washington's business.
Redirect federal addiction-recovery funding in our region away from secular clinics and give it directly to faith-based pastoral counseling centers.
We want to entirely defund the federal Department of Agriculture's oversight boards. We know how to farm. We don't need to pay coastal salaries to tell us how to plant wheat.
We've convinced three more tech conglomerates to move their headquarters from California to Austin. We just need a federal tax holiday to seal the deal.
The federal energy grid mandates are a bureaucratic nightmare. Sign this waiver permanently exempting the Texas power grid from federal oversight.
Global energy demands are skyrocketing. Expedite the permits for four new mega-LNG (Liquid Natural Gas) export terminals on the Gulf Coast. Ignore the EPA delays.
To remain globally competitive, we are outlawing federal union-protection laws within Texas state lines. The National Labor Relations Board has no jurisdiction here.
Private space exploration is the future. Give Texas a blanket federal waiver for rocket launch emissions and airspace closures over the Gulf.
Border security is draining our budget. Let us contract it out to private security firms. They're cheaper, faster, and won't ask for federal pensions.
Chancellor, we need to abolish the federal corporate minimum tax. It's punishing our mega-refineries just because they know how to find write-offs.
Cut the federal funding for Texas highway maintenance. Give us the authority to sell the interstates to private toll-road operators. We'll turn traffic into profit.
We've discovered massive new oil reserves under the Permian Basin, but it requires aggressive fracking. Veto the federal groundwater protection acts so we can drill.
Cancel the federal antitrust investigations into the energy conglomerates based in Houston. Big business isn't a crime, it's the Texas model.
Cheap foreign steel is gutting our foundries. Slap a 40% federal tariff on all imported metals to protect Superior's union jobs, even if it starts a trade war.
Silicon Valley's 'gig economy' is just glorified wage theft. Pass a federal mandate forcing all tech apps to classify drivers as full employees with union rights.
Coastal emission mandates are going to wipe out a million combustion-engine jobs in Detroit. Give the auto industry a 10-year federal waiver to keep building gas cars.
Southern states are poaching our factories using union-busting 'Right-to-Work' laws. We need a federal ban on these laws to level the playing field for the working man.
Fund the Great Lakes Dredging Mega-Project. It will create fifty thousand union jobs and drastically increase our shipping capacity, but it requires a massive federal deficit spend.
Robots don't pay taxes or buy groceries. We want a federal 'Automation Penalty' on corporations that replace human assembly-line workers with robotics.
We have investors ready to reopen massive abandoned steel mills, but the EPA's 'Brownfield' cleanup regulations are blocking it. Waive the environmental red tape so we can get to work.
Chancellor, issue a 'Buy American' executive order. The Pentagon must source 100% of its vehicles, steel, and armaments from domestic union shops in Superior.
The time has come. Back our federal mandate for a 32-hour workweek with no loss in pay for industrial workers. The corporations have been hoarding profits for decades; they can afford it.
If foreign countries want to sell goods in our markets, make them prove their workers are paid American minimum wages. Pass the Global Fair Wage Tariff.
Chancellor, the 'green transition' is starving our towns. I need a federal executive order to reopen the sealed mountain coal mines and classify coal as a vital national asset.
Our local school boards voted to mandate daily Christian prayer and traditional moral instruction in the classroom. Tell the federal courts to back off.

Megacorporations are bleeding our workers dry with 24/7 shifts. We want a federal exemption to reinstate strict 'Blue Laws'—banning all corporate business on Sundays to honor the Sabbath and give folks a rest.
The Black Lung epidemic is ravaging our elders. We demand a massive, permanent federal pension fund for all retired mountain workers, paid for by Wall Street.
Wall Street real estate trusts are buying up family farms and turning our hollows into luxury retreats. Pass a federal ban on corporate land hoarding in Appalachia.
Coastal tech firms are deploying fleets of self-driving long-haul trucks, destroying the livelihoods of a million American truckers. Ban autonomous trucking on federal interstates.
The opioid crisis is killing our youth. The federal secular clinics just pass out synthetic substitutes. We need those funds redirected entirely to localized, faith-based rehabilitation ministries.
Foreign conglomerates are buying up our historic textile mills and gutting them for parts. Give me the federal authority to seize these mills and hand them over to worker co-ops.
Washington wants to use eminent domain to turn a million acres of Appalachian logging land into a 'National Green Reserve'. Veto the land grab. Our people need the timber.
Mega-agricultural corporations in the Midwest are drowning our small, family-owned tobacco and hemp farms with under-priced goods. We need a domestic subsidy specifically for micro-farms.
Maintaining federal data centers is too expensive. Allow us to privatize the NSA's domestic servers to defense contractors in Virginia. It balances the budget and expands our surveillance net.
Chancellor, we need to fund the next generation of stealth bombers. I propose slashing federal education grants to the Northeast to cover the manufacturing costs in Maryland.
The FBI needs broader authority to monitor and infiltrate radical labor unions in the Rust Belt. We believe they are threatening critical supply chains.
Federal pensions are bloated. Let me freeze all hiring and slash retirement benefits for non-essential civil servants across the country. We need the capital for defense, not retirees.
Unregulated militias out West are a direct threat to the state. Authorize a massive ATF crackdown to seize unregistered weapons. We'll fine them heavily to offset the operation costs.
The Pentagon requires a new East Coast Cyber-Warfare Command Center. The cheapest location is right on top of protected wetlands in Delaware. Waive the environmental act.
Physical border walls are an antiquated waste of money. Cancel Mesa's concrete contracts and redirect the budget to Commonwealth aerospace firms for orbital surveillance satellites.
Foreign tech components in our defense grid are a vulnerability. Ban all non-domestic hardware in federal contracts. It will hurt the free market, but keep the empire secure.
Local police forces are disjointed and expensive. Let the DHS absorb them. We will standardize their training, cut local redundancies, and federalize the law.
I need a federal mandate to protect the Delaware corporate tax havens. Our defense contractors rely on those loopholes to stay globally dominant. Do not let Congress audit them.
The UN requires expanding their New York headquarters. Give them total tax exemption and federalize the surrounding Manhattan blocks to spare the city the expense.
Chancellor, 'Buy American' provisions are inefficient and violate our World Trade Organization treaties. Repeal them. We should buy steel from whichever country is cheapest.
We must meet our international foreign aid commitments. Cut federal agricultural welfare in the Midwest to fund the new Global Development Bank.
To balance the budget, we are proposing the privatization of the Northeast rail corridor. A consortium of European sovereign wealth funds is ready to buy it today.
Our Ivy League institutions are vital global assets. Grant them a permanent federal exemption from all endowment taxes. We can't let populists raid our intellectual capital.
Wall Street requires frictionless international capital flow. Sign the 'Open Markets Treaty' to remove all federal oversight on foreign corporate mergers in the Union.
Lift the federal caps on H-1B visas for international finance and tech workers. Let's insource the best global minds and let the domestic workforce adapt or fail.
The federal shipbuilding subsidies in the South are a waste. End them. We can buy European and Asian commercial vessels for a fraction of the cost.
We want to join the Global Emissions Coalition. However, to maintain fiscal neutrality, we must implement a sweeping regressive carbon tax on domestic consumer goods.
Austerity is necessary, Chancellor. We are slashing the federal budget for industrial worker retraining programs to redirect funds to the International Monetary Fund (IMF).
Three European automakers want to build massive plants in Piedmont. We need federal funding to fast-track the infrastructure, but guarantee these will be non-union shops.
The Research Triangle is starved for talent. We need you to triple the regional quota for international tech visas, regardless of the Pentagon's 'border security' complaints.
Charlotte is ready to overtake New York as the premier global banking hub. We just need the Treasury to loosen foreign investment regulations exclusively for our financial district.
To attract international media conglomerates to our film industry, we need to pass federal protections against local censorship laws. The Deep South won't like it, but the money is astronomical.
We must dredge the Port of Savannah to handle the new mega-freighters from Asia. Fast-track the permits and bypass the federal environmental reviews to keep global supply chains moving.
Piedmont is negotiating a direct agricultural export pipeline to China. We need the federal government to subsidize our corporate mega-farms so we can price out the international competition.
Atlanta is bidding to host a massive international peace and trade summit. It will cost billions in federal security and infrastructure funds, but it makes us the center of the global economy.
Allow Piedmont to establish a 'Federal Free Trade Zone' in our coastal cities. Zero federal tariffs, zero import taxes. It will siphon shipping away from the Gulf, but our GDP will soar.
An international tech consortium wants to buy a massive stake in our regional telecom infrastructure. The Pentagon claims it's a security risk. Override them. We need the capital.
We want to launch a federally backed 'Global Talent Pipeline' targeting executives and scientists worldwide to relocate to Piedmont. We will offer them zero federal income tax for 5 years.
The Gulf Coast is ready to boom. Waive the federal environmental protections on offshore drilling so we can triple our deep-water oil rig deployment.
Corporate 'ESG' and diversity mandates are poison to our traditional business culture. Give us a federal waiver to heavily tax any corporation operating in Dixie that enforces them.
Federal labor boards are trying to force union elections in our massive auto and aerospace plants. Issue an executive order cementing 'Right-to-Work' supremacy on all federal contracts.
Public schools are failing our children's moral education. Redirect federal education block grants in Dixie away from public districts and directly into private, faith-based charter schools.
The sugar and citrus industries are suffocating under federal wetland restoration acts. Stop the federal draining mandates and let our agricultural titans reclaim the swamps.
Foreign conglomerates are buying up our legacy farmland. Pass a federal ban preventing any non-citizen entity from purchasing agricultural acreage in the Deep South.

Our coastal tourism industry thrives on a family-friendly atmosphere. We are passing regional laws to ban progressive coastal cruise lines from docking unless they adhere to traditional indecency standards.
The hospitality and harvest sectors need hands, not red tape. Relax the federal seasonal worker wage floors and child-labor restrictions. Our family businesses need flexibility.
The federal 'Sin Taxes' on tobacco, alcohol, and regional gambling are punishing our heritage industries. Abolish the federal excise taxes and let the states manage their own vices.
Federal agencies are mandating gender-neutral facilities in all federal buildings within our state lines. Veto this mandate. We will not spend taxpayer capital on coastal social engineering.
The Arctic is thawing, opening up billions in deep-sea oil. I need the federal government to waive all wildlife protections so we can deploy the first wave of mega-rigs before foreign rivals do.
We are sitting on the Bering Strait, staring down our adversaries. Fund the construction of a massive, heavily armed federal icebreaker fleet to secure the northern trade routes.
Rare-earth minerals are essential for national defense, and they are buried under indigenous lands in Alaska. We need federal clearance to use eminent domain and bypass local equity councils.
I want to permanently station nuclear-powered attack submarines in our pristine coastal fjords. It will industrialize the coastline, but the Pacific requires a show of force.
Extreme cold-weather drilling requires non-stop shifts that violate federal labor safety hours. Grant our mega-corporations an exemption so we don't halt production in the dark months.
The Pentagon wants to test experimental, high-altitude EMP weaponry over the Alaskan tundra. It might occasionally knock out civilian grids, but we need the federal defense contracts.
To rapidly scale up our mineral extraction, I want to sell massive tracts of federal land in Alaska directly to foreign multinational conglomerates. Mesa hates foreigners, but the cash is too good.
We need to expand the federal military reserve training zones. I propose we seize control of millions of acres of prime Alaskan hunting and fishing territory, closing it off to the public.
The commercial salmon fishing industry is collapsing. Let's abandon the conservation quotas. Let the mega-trawlers scrape the bottom until it's gone, then we'll pivot to offshore oil.
The Northern border is too vast to patrol with men. I want federal funding to construct an autonomous, lethal drone-surveillance grid across the entire Alaskan perimeter.

The Pacific fleet is poisoning our waters. We demand the federal government close the Red Hill fuel facility immediately and begin the decades-long process of returning Pearl Harbor to indigenous stewardship.
Overtourism is destroying our reefs and culture. We want federal authorization to impose a hard cap on commercial flights to the islands and institute a massive 'Eco-Tax' on mainland tourists.
Mainland billionaires and global hedge funds are buying up our islands, pricing locals into homelessness. Pass a federal ban preventing non-residents from purchasing residential property in Hawaii.
Industrial fishing fleets from the mainland are strip-mining the Pacific. We demand a federally enforced, 500-mile 'No-Take' marine sanctuary around the entire Hawaiian archipelago.
We are establishing the Sovereign Hawaiian Tribunal to arbitrate internal land disputes using traditional indigenous law. We just need the federal courts to formally cede their jurisdiction.

We want to completely sever Hawaii from imported fossil fuels. Give us a massive federal grant to build community-owned, decentralized solar and geothermal grids, cutting out the corporate energy giants.
The historical erasure of our people ends now. Mandate 'Ōlelo Hawai'i (the Hawaiian language) as a fully funded co-equal language in all federal operations, schools, and broadcasts in the Pacific.

As sea levels rise, we are facing an influx of Pacific Island climate refugees. We need the Treasury to establish a 'Climate Reparations Fund' to house them, paid for by a tax on mainland carbon emitters.
Silicon Valley wants to build giant server farms on sacred volcanic mountains to cool their AI mainframes. I will literally stand in front of the bulldozers. Revoke their federal land-use permits.

Our local agriculture has been dominated by mainland mega-corporations for over a century. Use federal antitrust laws to break up the plantation monopolies and redistribute the land to local farm co-ops.
